content.rendered:
Here’s a timely cover: Three times only, on the original River tour, Bruce and the E Street Band covered the classic CCR anti-gun polemic, “Run Through the Jungle.”

Bruce’s take on the song is quieter, slower and more taut than the original, with a mid-song key change to escalate the tension. If he performed it today, he’d have to update the lyrics, too–we’re at 300 million guns today and counting.

Here’s Bruce’s first of only three performances ever of “Run Through the Jungle.”

Interestingly, Bruce’s version has extra verses, presumably written by him. Compare with the original Creedence Clearwater Revival version:

A couple of those new lyrics would turn up later in the year in a new song Bruce was working on but was never released: “Bells of San Salvador.”

But that’s a story for another time.

Run Through the Jungle
First performed: April 29, 1981 (Rotterdam, The Netherlands)
Last performed: May 8, 1981 (Stockholm, Sweden)

© April 5, 2018

excerpt.rendered: A quiet, taut take on the CCR anti-gun classic rocker. “200 millions guns are loaded…” If only.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Born on the Bayou
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce Springsteen and John Fogerty trade lead vocals on a smoking performance of "Born on the Bayou" at the 1993 Rock and Roll Hall of Fame induction.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice (Cover Me?): I’m a Coward
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Springsteen's "I'm a Coward." Homage or cover? You decide.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Mardi Gras: Land of Hope and Dreams
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Back in 2011, Rome-based Mardi Gras released a gorgeously uplifting cover of "Land of Hope and Dreams." Check it out inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Hey Tonight
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only (in public at least): Bruce Springsteen covers John Fogerty's 1971 hit single with John Eddie, Greg Kihn, Elliott Murphy, and Marshall Crenshaw.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Many Rivers to Cross
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Many Rivers to Cross" may not be Bruce's most well-known Jimmy Cliff cover, but it may be his most faithful.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Who’ll Stop the Rain
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce Springsteen's cover of this timeless CCR classic sparked a national conversation that never stopped.
